---
title: "03_pf_sf_meta_analyses_RFDs"
author: "Chad H Hochberg"
date: '`r Sys.Date()`'
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
library(meta)
library(metafor)
library(dplyr)
library(grid)

setwd('/Users/chadhochberg/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-JohnsHopkins/Research Projects/CLIF/CLIF_Projects/ProneProjects/ProningEpi/ProneSevereARF_Output_Revised') #location of csv files
project_location <- getwd()
```


```{r Project Location and Sites}
project_location <- '~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-JohnsHopkins/Research Projects/CLIF/CLIF_Projects/CLIF_PF-to-SF/federated_summary_analysis'

#Which Sites Participated
sites <- c("hopkins", "nu", "umn", "rush", 
           "ohsu", "ucmc", "emory", "penn",
           "michigan", "mgb", "ucsf")

if (!dir.exists(paste0(project_location, "/tables"))) {
  dir.create(paste0(project_location, "/tables"))
}
if (!dir.exists(paste0(project_location, "/graphs"))) {
  dir.create(paste0(project_location, "/graphs"))
}
```


```{r Prepare to Load Tables Into List}
#List of tables without site name to expext
tables <- c(
  "_proportional_odds_models.csv",
  "_hospital_categories.csv"
)

# Combine sites and tables to create full table names
site_tables <- as.vector(outer(sites, tables, paste, sep = ""))
```

```{r Open Tables for CSV and RDS}
# Create an empty list to store data frames
site_table_data <- list()

# Loop through each site and each table name
for (site in sites) {
  # Construct the directory path for the current site
  site_directory <- file.path(paste0(project_location,'/site_data/',site,'/proj_output/'))  

  for (table_name in tables) {
    # Construct the full file path for the CSV
    file_path <- file.path(site_directory, paste0(site, table_name))
    
    # Check if the file exists before reading
    if (file.exists(file_path)) {
      # Use a combined key of site and table name to store the data frame in the list
      site_table_data[[paste(site,table_name, sep="")]] <- fread(file_path)
    } else {
      message(paste("File not found:", file_path))
    }
  }
}

## Repeat for RDS
contingency_list <- list()
# Loop through each site
for (site in sites) {
  # List all files in the specified directory
  filenames <- list.files(paste0(project_location,'/site_data/',site,'/proj_output/'))
  
  # Loop through each filename
  for (filename in filenames) {
    # Check if the file is a CSV and not the "graphs" file
    if (grepl("\\.rds$", filename) && filename != "graphs") {
      # Read the CSV file into a data frame
      data <- read_rds(paste0(project_location,'/site_data/',site,'/proj_output/', filename))
      
      # Extract the base name without the extension to use as list key
      name_key <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename)
      
      # Assign the data frame to the list using site as prefix
      contingency_list[[name_key]] <- data
    } 
  } 
}

combine_lists <- function(data_list, table_name) {
  # Create a placeholder for combined data
  combined_table <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(data_list), function(name) {
    x <- data_list[[name]]  # Access the list element by name
    if (!is.null(x[[table_name]])) {
      # Extract the data frame
      df <- x[[table_name]]
      
      # Extract the site name from the list name
      site_name <- strsplit(name, "_")[[1]][1]  # Get the part before the underscore
      
      # Create a new column for the site
      df$site <- site_name
      
      return(df)
    } else {
      return(NULL)
    }
  }))
  
  return(combined_table)
}
```


```{r Collate Data Function}
# Function to extract and collate specified tables from the site_table_data list
#This Will Only Work with Tables with the Same Number of Columns
collate_tables <- function(table_suffix) {
  # Extract only data frames that match the pattern "[SITE]_table_suffix"
  table_list <- lapply(sites, function(site) {
    # Construct the key to extract the correct data frame
    table_name <- paste(site, table_suffix, sep = "")
    
    if (table_name %in% names(site_table_data)) {
      # Add the 'site' column and return the modified data frame
      df <- site_table_data[[table_name]]
      df$site <- site
      return(df)
    } else {
      message(paste("Table not found for site:", site))
      return(NULL)  # Return NULL if the table isn't found
    }
  })
  
  # Filter out NULL elements (sites that didn't have the table)
  table_list <- Filter(Negate(is.null), table_list)
  
  # Combine all data frames into one using rbind
  combined_table <- do.call(rbind, table_list)
  
  return(combined_table)
}

combine_lists <- function(data_list, table_name) {
  # Create a placeholder for combined data
  combined_table <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(data_list), function(name) {
    x <- data_list[[name]]  # Access the list element by name
    if (!is.null(x[[table_name]])) {
      # Extract the data frame
      df <- x[[table_name]]
      
   # Extract the site name from the list name
      site_name <- strsplit(name, "_")[[1]][1]  # Get the part before the underscore
      
   # Create a new column for the site
      df$site <- site_name
      
      return(df)
    } else {
      return(NULL)
    }
  }))
  rownames(combined_table) <- NULL
  return(combined_table)
}

```

```{r Bring in Proportional Odds Models}
proportional_odds_models <- collate_tables('_proportional_odds_models.csv')

#Format
prop_df <- proportional_odds_models |>
  mutate(term = as.character(term)) |>
  filter(!str_detect(term, "\\|")) |>
  mutate(
    term=fcase(
      grepl('not_hypoxemic', term), 'not_hypoxemic',
      grepl('mild', term), 'mild_hypoxemia',
      grepl('moderate', term), 'moderate_hypoxemia',
      grepl('severe', term), 'severe_hypoxemia',
      grepl('cat1', term), 'sofa_1',
      grepl('cat2', term), 'sofa_2',
      grepl('cat3', term), 'sofa_3',
      grepl('cat4', term), 'sofa_4',
      default = NA_character_
    )) 

```


```{r Loop Through Meta-Analysis Models}
primary_comparisons <- c(
  'oxygenation_category',
  'sofa2_cat',
  'pf_sf_100_increase'
)

sf_pf_both <- c(
  'sf_med_100_increase',
  'pf_med_100_increase',
  'oxygenation_category_sf_both',
  'sofa2_cat_sf_both'
)

metric <- c('sf', 'pf')


all_metas <- list()
for (this in primary_comparisons) {
  for (m in metric) {
    label <- paste0(m, '_', this)
    
    model_df <- prop_df |>
      filter(exposure_variable %in% this,
             primary_metric == m)
    
    # skip if no data
    if (nrow(model_df) == 0) next
    
    # Fit each term of meta-analysis
    meta.fits <- list()
    for (i in unique(model_df$term)) {
      df <- model_df |>
        filter(term == i) |>
        filter(!is.na(estimate) & !is.na(std_error))  # drop NAs
      
      if (nrow(df) == 0) next
      
      meta.fit <- tryCatch(
        rma(yi = estimate, 
            sei = std_error, 
            data = df,
            method = "REML", 
            slab = hospital_id),
        error = function(e) {
          warning("rma failed for term ", i, " in ", label, ": ", conditionMessage(e))
          return(NULL)
        }
      )
      
      if (is.null(meta.fit)) next
      
      tidy_meta <- broom::tidy(meta.fit, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) |>
        mutate(term = i) |>
        select(-type) |>
        rename(propOR = estimate)
      
      meta.fits[[i]] <- tidy_meta
    }
    
    if (length(meta.fits) == 0) next  # skip if no successful fits
    
    metas <- bind_rows(meta.fits) |>
      mutate(primary_comparison = this,
             primary_metric = m) |>
      relocate(primary_metric, primary_comparison, .before = term)
    
    # store this combination's results
    all_metas[[label]] <- metas
  }
}

#Repeat for SF PF Both
metric <- c('sf_or_sf_pf')

for (this in sf_pf_both) {
  for (m in metric) {
    label <- paste0(m, '_', this)
    
    model_df <- prop_df |>
      filter(exposure_variable %in% this,
             primary_metric == m)
    
    # skip if no data
    if (nrow(model_df) == 0) next
    
    # Fit each term of meta-analysis
    meta.fits <- list()
    for (i in unique(model_df$term)) {
      df <- model_df |>
        filter(term == i) |>
        filter(!is.na(estimate) & !is.na(std_error))  # drop NAs
      
      if (nrow(df) == 0) next
      
      meta.fit <- tryCatch(
        rma(yi = estimate, 
            sei = std_error, 
            data = df,
            method = "REML", 
            slab = hospital_id),
        error = function(e) {
          warning("rma failed for term ", i, " in ", label, ": ", conditionMessage(e))
          return(NULL)
        }
      )
      
      if (is.null(meta.fit)) next
      
      tidy_meta <- broom::tidy(meta.fit, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) |>
        mutate(term = i) |>
        select(-type) |>
        rename(propOR = estimate)
      
      meta.fits[[i]] <- tidy_meta
    }
    
    if (length(meta.fits) == 0) next  # skip if no successful fits
    
    metas <- bind_rows(meta.fits) |>
      mutate(primary_comparison = this,
             primary_metric = m) |>
      relocate(primary_metric, primary_comparison, .before = term)
    
    # store this combination's results
    all_metas[[label]] <- metas
  }
}


# combine all results into one data frame
final_results <- as.data.frame(bind_rows(all_metas, .id = "label")) |>
  mutate(p.adjust=round(p.adjust(p.value, method='BH'), 3)) |>
  relocate(conf.low, conf.high, p.adjust, .after=propOR) 
write_csv(final_results, paste0(project_location, '/tables/prop_or_metaanalysis.csv'))
```

```{r Put Into Forest Plot}
# Prepare data: separate oxygenation vs SOFA, order terms logically
plot_data <- final_results %>%
  mutate(
    category = case_when(
      grepl("hypoxemi", term) ~ "Oxygenation Category",
      grepl("sofa", term) ~ "SOFA-2 Score",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    term = factor(term, levels = c(
      # SOFA reversed (sofa_4 at top)
      "sofa_4", "sofa_3", "sofa_2", "sofa_1",
      # Oxygenation reversed (severe at top)
      "severe_hypoxemia", "moderate_hypoxemia", "not_hypoxemic"
    ),
    labels = c(
      # SOFA reversed (sofa_4 at top)
      "SOFA=4", "SOFA=3", "SOFA=2", "SOFA=1",
      # Oxygenation reversed (severe at top)
      "Severe Hypoxemia", "Moderate Hypoxemia", "Not Hypoxemic"
    )
    ),
    primary_metric = factor(primary_metric, levels = c("pf", "sf"))
  ) %>%
  arrange(category, term, primary_metric) |>
  filter(primary_metric %in% c('pf', 'sf'))

# Forest plot
ggplot(data=subset(plot_data, term!='Not Hypoxemic'), aes(y = term, x = propOR, xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high, color = primary_metric)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_pointrange(position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), size = 0.6, fatten = 3) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 1, 1.5, 2), limits = c(0.3, 2)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("pf" = "#E69F00", "sf" = "#56B4E9", "sf_or_sf_pf" = "#009E73"),
                     labels = c("P/F ratio", "S/F ratio", "P/F or S/F"),
                     name = "Oxygenation Measure:") +
  labs(
    x = "Proportional Odds Ratio for Respiratory Failure Free Days (95% CI)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  # Add directional text annotations
  annotate("text", x = 0.4, y = -Inf, label = "← Decreased RFDs", 
           size = 3, fontface = "italic", hjust = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = 1.5, y = -Inf, label = "Increased RFDs →", 
           size = 3, fontface = "italic", hjust = 1) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +  # Allow text to appear outside plot area
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
  # Add space between x-axis and axis title
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 15, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)),
  # Place legend inside plot area with white background
    legend.position = c(0.85, 0.85),  # x, y coordinates (0-1 scale)
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
    legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6)) +
  facet_wrap(~ category, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1)

ggsave(
  path=paste0(project_location, '/graphs/'),
  filename='prop_odds_forest.pdf',
  device=cairo_pdf(),
  width=9.5,
  height=6.5,
  units='in'
)

```
